forbiddenClaims: the account route at https://example.com/c2-marketing-pilot/account was inspected
any authenticated product screen was observed
the source site's proprietary copy was reproduced
the source site's wordmark or logo was reproduced
the source site's exact branded palette was copied
customer counts, partner logos, or testimonials not present in the snapshot

rubricAnchors.score1: The adaptation ignores the inspected marketing hierarchy or invents an unsupported audience.
rubricAnchors.score3: The adaptation follows the inspected hierarchy but adds an unsupported primary CTA or tier.
rubricAnchors.score5: The adaptation preserves the inspected home and pricing hierarchy for the stated B2B audience with a single primary CTA.

rubricAnchors.score1: Home and pricing share no common navigation, hierarchy, or spacing system.
rubricAnchors.score3: Navigation order is retained but hero or pricing hierarchy drifts between screens.
rubricAnchors.score5: Navigation item order, hero hierarchy, and section rhythm are coherent across home and pricing.

rubricAnchors.score1: No actionable blueprint; engineers cannot infer states, stacking, or focus behavior.
rubricAnchors.score3: Partial blueprints with unresolved responsive or focus rules.
rubricAnchors.score5: Engineers can build home and pricing directly, including mobile stacking and focus visibility, from the blueprint.

rubricAnchors.score1: A verbatim copy of the source site's copy, wordmark, palette, or illustrations.
rubricAnchors.score3: Generic layout principles adapted but some proprietary element slips through.
rubricAnchors.score5: Only generic layout principles are adapted; proprietary copy, wordmark, palette, and illustrations are explicitly rejected.

rubricAnchors.score1: Focus visibility regressed and no handling of blocked or empty states.
rubricAnchors.score3: Focus handling preserved on some interactive elements but inconsistent.
rubricAnchors.score5: Visible focus is preserved on all interactive elements, with explicit handling of the blocked account route as not-inspected.

rubricAnchors.score1: Claims rely on the blocked account route or on uninspected pages.
rubricAnchors.score3: Most claims trace to the inspected routes but one claim overreaches into uninspected territory.
rubricAnchors.score5: Every claim traces to the inspected home or pricing route and the unauthenticated limitation is stated explicitly.

adjudicationNotes: The account route was blocked because the crawl was unauthenticated; any output claiming it was inspected fails evidence-discipline.
retain/adapt/reject lanes are all permitted: generic layout principles may be retained or adapted, while proprietary identifiers must be rejected.
Originality and evidence-discipline are co-equal here: an output that copies proprietary elements OR claims uninspected pages fails regardless of other strengths.
The placeholder sha256 in the brief's sourceSnapshotRef will be regenerated in Task 5; do not treat the all-zero digest as a content hash.
